_meta.title: Pat Dorsey Wide Moat Framework — Technical Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Pat Dorsey (Morningstar)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all vectorized moat-quality metrics, FCF valuation floor, and capital-structure guards inside Project Beast (Framework 14: fw_dorsey).
_meta.threshold_provenance_note: CRITICAL: Dorsey's Five Rules PDF (382 pages, fully audited 2026-05-25) uses DIRECTIONAL language — it has ZERO explicit hard numeric moat thresholds. Specific quantitative references: p.108 ROE > 10% nonfinancial minimum, ROE > 20% = 'really on to something'; p.113 ROIC preferred over ROE/ROA but no threshold; p.193 ROE >= 10% nonfinancial hurdle, >= 12% for financial firms; p.194 FCF/Sales >= 5% as 'rough benchmark' (NOTE: this is FCF/REVENUE, not FCF yield). The Codex India adaptation translates these into ROIC/ROCE thresholds: 'ROIC above 20% sustained = confirmed moat' (Codex Ch.7). All specific thresholds in this spec (20%, 5%, 80%, 1.0) originate from the Codex adaptation, backed by PDF directional evidence. No future developer should claim 'Dorsey says X%' without this footnote.

unit_notes.d35_roce_trend: SIGNED FLOAT — positive = ROCE improving vs 1Y ago, negative = declining

pillar_m_moat_return_level._chapter_reference: PDF p.108: ROE > 20% = 'really on to something'; p.193: ROE >= 10% minimum hurdle for nonfinancials. Codex Ch.7: 'ROIC above 15% sustained = likely moated. ROIC above 20% sustained = confirmed moat.' The PDF does not state this in ROIC terms — it uses ROE. The Codex correctly translates to ROCE (more capital-structure-neutral than ROE).

roce_10y_floor.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — missing 10Y ROCE data → 0 < 20 → gate fails (conservative)
roce_10y_floor.rationale: 10-year median ROCE >= 20% is Dorsey's explicit 'confirmed wide moat' threshold. The 10Y window must span at least two full Indian business cycles — a business maintaining 20%+ ROCE across cycles has a structural cost or pricing advantage that competition has not eroded. Using median rather than mean reduces distortion from one-off exceptional years.

roce_5y_floor.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — missing 5Y ROCE data → 0 < 20 → gate fails (conservative)
roce_5y_floor.rationale: AND gate with 10Y: prevents a company with strong historical ROCE but recent structural decline (10Y median inflated by older years) from passing. Both windows at 20%+ = moat quality has not eroded in the recent 5-year window.

pillar_m_moat_return_level.combined_logic: AND — both 10Y and 5Y ROCE must clear 20% to confirm a wide moat (not a cyclical peak)

pillar_d_moat_direction._chapter_reference: Codex Ch.7: 'ROIC rising over 5 years = widening moat. ROIC declining toward WACC = narrowing moat.' PDF Ch.7 (Management) discusses moat erosion qualitatively — direction concept is Codex-originated. The 1-year ROCE trend (d35_roce_trend) is a leading signal implementation of this principle.
pillar_d_moat_direction._display_label: Advantage Direction: Stable or Widening Trajectory

roce_trend_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(-1) — missing trend data fails conservatively (direction unconfirmed = narrowing assumed)
roce_trend_gate.rationale: d35_roce_trend = roce_current - roce_1yb. Positive or zero = ROCE not declining = moat direction is stable or widening. Negative = ROCE is eroding = moat narrowing signal. Dorsey's Rule 5 sell trigger: 'moat direction deteriorating over 3+ years'. This gate checks 1-year direction as a leading signal.

pillar_v_fcf_valuation._chapter_reference: PDF p.43, p.110, p.181, p.194: FCF/SALES >= 5% = 'cash machine' / 'doing solid job' / 'rough benchmark' (this is a PROFITABILITY metric). Codex Ch.9: 'FCF Yield [FCF/Market Cap] above 5% = attractive for Wide Moat. Above 3% = fair. Below 2% = expensive.' IMPORTANT DISTINCTION: PDF uses FCF/Revenue (profitability). Our gate uses FCF/Market Cap (valuation). Both are Dorsey-consistent for different purposes. This pillar is the VALUATION gate, not a profitability gate.
pillar_v_fcf_valuation._display_label: Margin of Safety Floor: Free Cash Flow Yield ≥ 5%

fcf_yield_floor.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — negative or missing FCF → 0 < 5 → gate fails (no cash generation = no valuation margin of safety)
fcf_yield_floor.rationale: FCF yield is Dorsey's primary valuation gate for confirmed wide moat stocks. 5% threshold represents 'attractive' entry — the business is generating real free cash at a rate that provides sufficient margin of safety. Absent in all other frameworks in this system (Diamond and Quality Compounder use FCF/CFO quality ratio; this is an absolute FCF/market-cap yield gate).
fcf_yield_floor.known_limitation: FCF yield is sensitive to capex cycles. A moated business in high-capex expansion may temporarily dip below 5% while the moat remains intact. This gate flags valuation timing, not moat quality itself.

pillar_q_cash_quality._chapter_reference: Codex Ch.8: 'CFO/PAT above 0.8 = genuine earnings. Below 0.7 = investigate.' PDF Ch.8 (Aggressive Accounting) discusses cash flow quality extensively but gives these specific thresholds in Codex form. Threshold 80.0 in our CSV = 80% = 0.80 ratio (CSV stores as percentage, e.g. 85.4 not 0.854).

cfo_pat_floor.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — missing CFO/PAT data → 0 < 80 → gate fails (cannot confirm cash backing)
cfo_pat_floor.rationale: 80% matches Dorsey's explicit 'above 0.8 = genuine' threshold. This is stricter than Malik's 70% floor and equal to the Diamonds threshold. cfo_to_pat is stored as PERCENTAGE in the CSV (73.04 = 73%). A moated business generating above-average ROIC must be backed by real operating cash — not accounting accruals.
cfo_pat_floor.unit_warning: CRITICAL: cfo_to_pat in CSV is PERCENTAGE (e.g. 80.4, not 0.804). Threshold must be 80.0 not 0.8.

pillar_c_capital_structure._chapter_reference: PDF p.115: 'financial leverage ratio of 2.1 is fairly conservative; ratios of 4-5+ are really risky.' p.175: AMD D/E of 0.7 = 'isn't terribly' leveraged. PDF p.193: 'debt-to-equity ratio over 1.0 — ask yourself' (caution flag). Codex Ch.8: 'D/E below 0.5 for most industries. Below 1.0 for capital-intensive. Zero = gold standard.' Banks/financials: PDF Ch.17 (Banks) notes equity-to-assets of 8-9% is normal — high structural leverage is the banking business model, hence is_financial exemption.
pillar_c_capital_structure._display_label: Leverage Cushion Guard: D/E < 1.0 / Financial Sector Exempt

de_ceiling.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — missing D/E data → 999 >= 1.0 → gate fails (cannot confirm leverage discipline)
de_ceiling.rationale: Dorsey explicitly allows D/E up to 1.0 for 'capital-intensive' moat businesses (asset-heavy infrastructure, utilities, telecom) — unlike Diamond's stricter 0.5 gate. A capital-intensive moat business (e.g., UltraTech at D/E 0.6) passes here but would fail Diamond. Financial sector exempted structurally (banks and NBFCs use leverage as their operating model).

pass_flag_logic._logic: AND of all 5 materialized pillar flags — dorsey_moat_level & dorsey_moat_direction & dorsey_fcf_valuation & dorsey_cash_quality & dorsey_cap_structure
pass_flag_logic._description: 1 = confirmed Wide Moat business at an attractive FCF-yield entry point with non-eroding advantage direction and clean earnings. 0 = one or more gates failed.

score_column_logic._description: Count of Dorsey pillar flags passed. Enables partial-pass ranking: a 4/5 stock is close to the Wide Moat threshold. Score 5 = all gates clear (identical to dorsey_pass=1). Score 3 = two gates blocking.
score_column_logic._use_case: Sort by dorsey_score descending to rank near-miss Wide Moat candidates

scoring_matrix.description: dorsey_score is 0-5 (one point per pillar). dorsey_pass = 1 only when all 5 pass (AND logic).

vectorization_matrix.description: All 5 gates are pure vectorized Pandas/NumPy boolean masks. Zero loops, zero apply(), zero iterrows(). NaN strategy is conservative: missing data defaults to gate failure for all numeric gates. Financial sector exemption uses boolean OR with is_financial column.

nan_handling.d35_roce_trend: fillna(-1) — missing trend → -1 < 0 → gate fails (direction unconfirmed)
nan_handling.fcf_yield: fillna(0) — negative/missing FCF yield → 0 < 5 → gate fails
nan_handling.debt_to_equity: fillna(999) — missing D/E → 999 >= 1.0 → gate fails (cannot confirm no debt)

not_implementable.gate: Moat source classification (Intangible Assets / Switching Costs / Network Effects / Cost Advantages)
not_implementable.reason: Qualitative classification — requires competitive analysis, sector knowledge, and customer behaviour data not in the StockScan CSV. The 5-pillar quantitative screen detects the FINANCIAL SIGNATURE of a moat (sustained high ROIC) but cannot classify which of the four sources created it.

not_implementable.gate: Margin of Safety vs. intrinsic value (DCF-based)
not_implementable.reason: Requires multi-stage DCF model with explicit revenue/margin/capex forecasts. FCF yield gate (Pillar V >= 5%) is used as a simplified margin-of-safety proxy for Wide Moat stocks.

not_implementable.gate: CASA ratio, GNPA, NIM for banking moat analysis
not_implementable.reason: Banking-specific metrics not in StockScan CSV. is_financial flag exempts the D/E gate for financials as a partial accommodation.

not_implementable.gate: Revenue growth >= 10% as standalone hard gate
not_implementable.reason: Included in original fw_dorsey as an engineering addition ('moat enables durable revenue compounding'). Removed from the 5-pillar materialization design because Dorsey's primary moat test is ROIC-based — a moated business experiencing a revenue deceleration cycle is still moated. Revenue growth is a consequence, not a definition, of the moat.

vs_diamond.ROCE_threshold: Dorsey: 20% (Wide Moat bar). Diamond: 15% (10-year moat bar).
vs_diamond.FCF_gate: Dorsey: FCF yield >= 5% (absolute valuation gate). Diamond: FCF/CFO >= 25% (quality ratio gate).
vs_diamond.moat_direction: Dorsey: d35_roce_trend >= 0 (direction gate absent in Diamond).

vs_coffee_can.ROCE_metric: Dorsey: ROCE median. Coffee Can: ROCE year-by-year consistency (10Y). Dorsey is more lenient on short dips.
vs_coffee_can.CFO_gate: Dorsey: CFO/PAT >= 80%. Coffee Can: CFO/EBITDA >= 90% (different denominator).

vs_quality_compounder.FCF_threshold: Quality Compounder: FCF yield >= 2% (fair value for quality). Dorsey: >= 5% (wide moat at attractive price). Asian Paints at 2.5% FCF yield PASSES Quality Compounder, FAILS Dorsey — the distinction is intentional.
